title: The Autopsy Report
content:
It is a sterile, official document:
POST-MORTEM EXAMINATION REPORT
Case Ref: MET-2026-9941X
Deceased: Vane, Thomas
EXTERNAL EXAMINATION:
Trauma: Single close-contact gunshot wound entry at the right temporal region. Minor subcutaneous ecchymosis (faint bruising) noted around the left volar wrist joint, measuring approximately 4cm. Dismissed as perimortem incidental contact.
INTERNAL EXAMINATION & BIOLOGICAL METRICS:
Liver Temperature: 29.4°C (84.9°F) recorded at scene arrival (09:15 AM).
Rigor Mortis: Fully established in upper and lower extremities.
Estimated Time of Death: Based on rate of algor mortis and metabolic decomposition, physiological death is estimated to have occurred between 21:30 and 22:30 (9:30 PM - 10:30 PM) on the preceding evening.
